Multi-Miner Template Conversion Guard

Release-candidate guard for the 2026-05-25 five-X100 efficiency regression. The RC must treat accepted block conversion, not raw hashes or connected miner count, as the mining success metric for active miner sources.

Invariant

Fresh installs assume zero miner sources. A deployment may later run 0..N ASIC or Stratum miner sources. Multi-miner pools, including five-X100 local pools, must have one canonical mining-template epoch at a time. Template refreshes, catch-up maintenance, and clean-job broadcasts must be atomic from the miner point of view. If old-parent, duplicate, or non-current submissions rise, the pool must repair the template epoch in code instead of relying on manual restarts.

Why

Multiple miner sources sharing one local pool can find block candidates faster than the pool can sometimes submit, observe a new DAG tip, invalidate old jobs, and deliver fresh jobs to every miner. Five ASICs made this visible because every accepted block invalidates five current jobs, but the same rule applies to one, two, or many active sources. Any backend churn or catch-up status churn during that transition turns useful hash into tip-overdue, duplicate-block-job, invalidated_job, or non_current_job loss.

RC Requirements

RequirementAcceptance Criteria
Zero-miner install defaultInstaller and first boot must start sync-only with zero miner sources. LAN scanning and miner configuration are explicit opt-in steps after initial install and sync.
Dynamic miner source countRelease readiness samples active miner windows using the observed/configured 0..N miner source count. A one-miner host is evaluated as one miner, and a five-miner host is evaluated as five miners.
Marginal lane-count proofDo not infer the optimum miner count from the dashboard per-miner Blocks plot alone. That plot is derived from a bounded recent pool-log window and can redistribute when a miner joins. Use direct pool counters, miner-hours, and a controlled A/B window before deciding that adding or removing a miner improves accepted block conversion.
Dashboard-independent measurementWhen the dashboard status API is slow or timing out, use direct Prometheus counter deltas such as ops/lane_efficiency_snapshot.py. The release evidence must include accepted blocks per miner-hour, rejected-per-accepted, local-rejected-per-accepted, share reject ratio, active miner count, and window duration.
Volatile lane attributionStratum job extranonce suffixes are current connection/lane state, not miner identity. Store only current-window suffixes, replace them on fresh authorize/subscribe evidence, and treat duplicate suffix ownership in the registry as ambiguous so the collector bootstraps from a larger log window instead of assigning work to the first MAC.
Inactive miner work isolationOffline or unmanaged ASIC observations may remain visible for operator context, but stale share windows must not receive active Work% or configured-lane status. Active lane balance is computed only from current/relevant MAC identities.
Template conversion SLORelease readiness fails if miner-normalized accepted block submits, tip-overdue loss, duplicate-local loss, stale-job rejects, or average submit/share latency exceed configured thresholds during active mining windows.
Node reorg/template churn gateWhen active miners are connected, repeated node Zero state root hash, Rewinding blockchain, Client in initial download, or recovered-template events must be counted as a mining-readiness risk even if block submission continues. A release gate must fail if this churn coincides with elevated tip-overdue, non_current_job, or template-conversion failure ratio.
Atomic template epochChanging template source state invalidates old jobs and broadcasts clean work. Template-fetch fallback cannot silently flip the mining lane.
Catch-up maintenance semanticsThe pool remains unavailable for paid mining until the active node is synced, template-safe, and submit-ready.
No topology quick fixDo not chase throughput by changing template-source topology unless a controlled A/B test proves miner-normalized accepted blocks improve for the target miner source count.
MAC-based miner diagnosticsPer-ASIC stale and duplicate attribution must use the MAC-address registry. IPs, ports, and worker labels remain ephemeral observations.

Backlog

Create or keep an SDLC backlog item for source-level prevention. The durable fix belongs in pool source and dashboard/sync-coordinator release gates, not only in watchdog repair scripts.

ItemAcceptance Criteria
RC-P0 volatile Stratum lane attributionRegression tests prove fresh authorize order overrides stale suffix registry state, ambiguous suffix ownership is not assigned to the first miner, mixed attributed/unattributed tails bootstrap from the larger log window, and inactive stale windows show 0.00 active Work%.
RC-P0 node churn to conversion-loss gateRelease readiness samples node logs and Prometheus together, then fails active-mining readiness when zero-state-root/rewind/template-initial-download churn appears with non-current share rejects, tip-overdue submits, or template-conversion failure above the configured SLO.
